item.title: Anxious Australian Retirees Ramp Up Pension Fund Withdrawals
item.description: Australian retirees are withdrawing their pension savings at increasing rates as global turmoil and surging inflation erodes their already fragile confidence.

item.title: Oil Steadies as the US and Iran Weigh Fresh Proposal to End War
item.description: Oil steadied after plunging 7% in the previous session, as the US and Iran weighed a fresh proposal to end the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Fertilizer Makers See Earnings Windfall as War Disrupts Supplies
item.description: Fertilizer makers CF Industries Holdings Inc. and Nutrien Ltd. each reported nearly 20% jumps in sales for the latest quarter, showing the extent of the Iran war’s upheaval on supply chains for the key crop nutrients.

item.title: China’s Midea Group Raises $2.2 Billion in Convertible Bond Sale
item.description: Chinese appliance maker Midea Group Co. raised $2.2 billion from the upsized sale of dual-tranche convertible bonds with zero coupon, according to terms of the deal seen by Bloomberg News.

item.title: Fortinet Jumps on Outlook Hike, Billings Growth Spurt
item.description: Fortinet Inc. shares rose as much as 24% in post-market trading after the cybersecurity company raised its full-year revenue outlook above expectations, following its largest billings expansion in over three years.

item.title: Albemarle Shares Jump as Lithium Recovery Delivers Earnings Beat
item.description: Albemarle Corp. shares surged after the lithium producer delivered better-than-expected quarterly results as higher prices and stronger volume lifted sales and earnings.

item.title: Blue Owl BDCs Buy Back $85 Million of Shares as Loan Values Sink
item.description: Two private credit funds managed by Blue Owl Capital Inc. bought back $85 million of shares as volatility in technology markets and a selloff in publicly traded loans brought down their value.

item.title: Zillow Shares Drop As Profit Outlook Miss Overshadows Upbeat 1Q
item.description: Zillow Group Inc. shares dropped 8% in post-market trading on Wednesday after the company’s second-quarter profit forecast missed Wall Street expectations, overshadowing upbeat results for the first quarter of the year.

item.title: Musk Weighed Offering Altman Tesla Board Seat, OpenAI Jury Told
item.description: Elon Musk at one point considered recruiting Sam Altman to serve on Tesla Inc.’s board of directors, jurors were told Wednesday at the trial over the feud between the two titans of artificial intelligence.

item.title: Apollo Private Credit Fund Reports Loss on Portfolio Weakness
item.description: A private credit fund overseen by Apollo Global Management Inc. reported a quarterly loss, citing declining valuations amid market volatility and weakness in some specific deals.

item.title: Pentagon’s Botched Blacklist Highlights Frail Trump-Xi Truce
item.description: A mishandled blacklisting of tech giants Alibaba and Baidu gives a rare window into an administration often at odds with itself on how to approach Beijing

item.title: Experian, Equifax Questioned by Warren About Buy Now, Pay Later
item.description: A group of Democratic senators pressed major credit bureaus about how the firms are incorporating data stemming from buy now, pay later loans into consumer credit reports.

item.title: JPMorgan-Led Group Eyes $500 Million Loss on Qualtrics Debt
item.description: A group of banks led by JPMorgan Chase & Co. is expected to shoulder paper losses of more than $500 million on a debt deal for software firm Qualtrics International Inc., according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Snap Says Advertising Revenue Buffeted by Effects of War in Iran
item.description: Snap Inc. projected sales in the current quarter that were in line with Wall Street estimates, while noting a key part of its business has been hampered by the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Meta-Backed Scale AI Wins $500 Million Defense Department Deal
item.description: The Pentagon awarded a $500 million contract to Meta Platforms Inc.-backed Scale AI to help sift through data and assist in decision-making, the latest step in the US military’s effort to rely more on artificial intelligence.

item.title: Vitol Is Turning to Mexico for Oil as War Disrupts Crude Market
item.description: Vitol Group is buying Mexican oil for the first time in a decade as the conflict in the Middle East roils global supply chains.

item.title: MetLife’s Improved PE Returns Lift Earnings Above Expectations
item.description: MetLife Inc.’s first-quarter earnings surpassed Wall Street expectations, as the insurer continued to cash in higher gains from its private equity portfolio.

item.title: DoorDash Gives a Forecast for Order Growth That Tops Estimates
item.description: DoorDash Inc. gave a forecast for order value in the current period that topped analyst estimates, signaling continued customer growth for the food-delivery company.

item.title: Arm Gains After Push Into AI Data Centers Bolsters Forecast
item.description: Arm Holdings Plc jumped in late trading after the company delivered a solid forecast and touted the demand for a new homegrown chip, signaling that a push into data center infrastructure is paying off.

item.title: Canada May ‘Redeploy’ Capital Tied up in Airports, Carney Says
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney said his government is considering spinning off or selling ownership stakes in Canadian airports in order to use the proceeds on new projects aimed at fueling economic growth.

item.title: Elite M&A Lawyers Fed Massive Insider-Trading Ring, US Alleges
item.description: Lawyers from top mergers and acquisitions firms provided tips on some of biggest deals of the last decade to an insider trading ring that made tens of millions of dollars in illegal profits, federal prosecutors said.

item.title: Fed’s Goolsbee Sounds Warning on Inflation, Consumer Behavior
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago President Austan Goolsbee struck a note of caution about inflation that has not only failed to continue cooling to the US central bank’s 2% target, but has moved up since the start of the US war in Iran.

item.title: Treasury Repo Trading Debate Spurs Big Wagers on Lending Rates
item.description: A discussion around the prospect of US Treasury investing its excess cash into short-term money markets unleashed a flurry of wagers betting on spread movements between overnight lending rates.

item.title: Gundlach Warns Investors Will Lose Money on Private Credit
item.description: DoubleLine Capital Chief Executive Officer Jeffrey Gundlach raised pointed questions about financial advisers and other intermediaries who ushered individual investors into private credit and other so-called semi-liquid funds, suggesting they’ve been motivated by high fees as much as by their clients’ interests.

item.title: Foreign Demand for Treasury Debt Is Stalling, Trade Group Says
item.description: Foreign investors are showing signs of diversifying away from US Treasuries as debt levels mount, according to the financial industry’s global trade group.

item.title: Canada’s Carbon Tax Hinders Pipeline Plans, Cenovus CEO Says
item.description: Alberta’s planned oil pipeline to the west coast requires Canada to shift away from stricter climate policies and toward promoting greater oil production from new projects, Cenovus Energy Inc.’s chief executive officer said.

item.title: Searchlight Capital’s Zinterhofer Sees Bifurcated M&A Market
item.description: Searchlight Capital Partners founding partner Eric Zinterhofer said he thinks dealmaking has bifurcated, with multiple buyers competing for good assets at high prices while less-viable companies are kept off the market.

item.title: FanDuel Names New Top Executive Amid Prediction Markets Push
item.description: Flutter Entertainment Plc elevated FanDuel President Christian Genetski to lead its US betting business, according to person familiar with the company’s plans.

item.title: Congo’s Biggest Lender Rawbank Posts 9% Rise in Annual Profit
item.description: Rawbank SA, the biggest lender in the Democratic Republic of Congo, saw after-tax profit increase at 9% to $232 million in 2025.

item.title: Biggest US Grid Needs Redesign to Cope With AI Boom, CEO Says
item.description: The biggest US power grid needs a revamp to cope with the unprecedented surge in electricity demand stemming from the data-center boom, said Chief Executive Officer David Mills.
